title: esus’ first recorded prayer part 2
scripture_text: “At that time Jesus said,“I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for this is what you were pleased to do. All things have been committed to me by my Father. No one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal him. “Come to me all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. for my yoke is easy and my burden is light.”
devotional:
The Invitation.

Before we can understand what Jesus is saying we need to do some background work. Many people feel that the burden Jesus was speaking of when he said 'Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened', was the burden of the law that the Pharisees had laid upon the people. The law was originally intended as the essentials necessary for the journey of faith by God's people. Unfortunately, over the years the children of Israel added to the list, adding much more than was necessary for the trip. For every "thou shalt" or "thou shalt not" they added many more commandments, so that the backpack grew in size to overpower those who had to carry it. By the time Jesus came along, it was too heavy for the journey. So Jesus wanted to remove the burden of the law from people.

Although this is a legitimate application of what Jesus is saying, I don't think this was his primary thought. In the context of Matthew 11, Jesus is not directly addressing the issue of legalism.

A second thing we need to understand is what Jesus meant by yoke. The yoke mentioned shouldn't be thought of as a wooden object, which was used to join two animals together, so that they might pull a plough or some other such agricultural device. Rather it was a wooden yoke placed over the shoulders of a single human being which was used to balance the weight being carried on two extremes, thus enabling the load to be laid upon the entire frame of the person.

A third factor is how the Old Testament uses the symbol of the yoke.

"This is what the Lord said to me; 'Make a yoke out of straps and crossbars and put it on your neck. Then send word to the kings of Edon, Moab, Ammon, Tyre and Sidon through the envoys who have come to Jerusalem to Zedekiah King of Judah. Give them this message for their masters and say. This is what the Lord almighty the God of Israel says. Tell this to your masters: with my great power I made the earth and its people and the animals that are on it, and I give it to anyone I please. Now I will hand all your countries over to my servant Nebuchadnezzar King of Babylon; I will even make the wild animals subject to him. All the nations will serve him and his son and his grandson until the time for his land comes; then many nations and great kings will subjugate him. If, however, any nation will not serve Nebuchadnezzar King of Babylon or bow its neck under his yoke, I will punish that nation with the sword, famine and plague declares the Lord, until I destroy it by his hand. So do not listen to your prophets, your diviners, your interpreters of dreams, your mediums or your sorcerers who tell you. 'You will not serve the King of Babylon,' They prophesy lies to you that will only serve to remove you far from your lands; I will banish you and you will perish. But if any nation will bow its neck under the yoke of the king of Babylon and serve him, I will let that nation remain in its own land, till it, and to live there declares the Lord. I gave the same message to Zedekiah King of Judah. Bow your neck under the yoke of the king of Babylon; serve him and his people and you will live." Jeremiah 27:1-12.

When God was judging the nations he placed a yoke on them as a form of punishment, this yoke implies the death of a way of life and the loss of a sense of identity.

When Jesus offered people his yoke, he was promising them more than a release from their heavy burdens, he was promising a new way of life and a new identity. So the yoke spoke about a persons identity and value system. The yoke is whatever controls and masters our lives.
prayer: Father help me be controlled and mastered by your yoke.
html:
esus’ first recorded prayer part 2

Matthew 11:25-30 J
“At that time Jesus said,“I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children. Yes, Father, for this is what you were pleased to do. All things have been committed to me by my Father. No one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal him. “Come to me all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. for my yoke is easy and my burden is light.”

The Invitation.

Before we can understand what Jesus is saying we need to do some background work. Many people feel that the burden Jesus was speaking of when he said 'Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened', was the burden of the law that the Pharisees had laid upon the people. The law was originally intended as the essentials necessary for the journey of faith by God's people. Unfortunately, over the years the children of Israel added to the list, adding much more than was necessary for the trip. For every "thou shalt" or "thou shalt not" they added many more commandments, so that the backpack grew in size to overpower those who had to carry it. By the time Jesus came along, it was too heavy for the journey. So Jesus wanted to remove the burden of the law from people.

Although this is a legitimate application of what Jesus is saying, I don't think this was his primary thought. In the context of Matthew 11, Jesus is not directly addressing the issue of legalism.

A second thing we need to understand is what Jesus meant by yoke. The yoke mentioned shouldn't be thought of as a wooden object, which was used to join two animals together, so that they might pull a plough or some other such agricultural device. Rather it was a wooden yoke placed over the shoulders of a single human being which was used to balance the weight being carried on two extremes, thus enabling the load to be laid upon the entire frame of the person.

A third factor is how the Old Testament uses the symbol of the yoke.

"This is what the Lord said to me; 'Make a yoke out of straps and crossbars and put it on your neck. Then send word to the kings of Edon, Moab, Ammon, Tyre and Sidon through the envoys who have come to Jerusalem to Zedekiah King of Judah. Give them this message for their masters and say. This is what the Lord almighty the God of Israel says. Tell this to your masters: with my great power I made the earth and its people and the animals that are on it, and I give it to anyone I please. If, however, any nation will not serve Nebuchadnezzar King of Babylon or bow its neck under his yoke, I will punish that nation with the sword, famine and plague declares the Lord, until I destroy it by his hand. So do not listen to your prophets, your diviners, your interpreters of dreams, your mediums or your sorcerers who tell you. 'You will not serve the King of Babylon,' They prophesy lies to you that will only serve to remove you far from your lands; I will banish you and you will perish. But if any nation will bow its neck under the yoke of the king of Babylon and serve him, I will let that nation remain in its own land, till it, and to live there declares the Lord. I gave the same message to Zedekiah King of Judah. Bow your neck under the yoke of the king of Babylon; serve him and his people and you will live." Jeremiah 27:1-12.

When God was judging the nations he placed a yoke on them as a form of punishment, this yoke implies the death of a way of life and the loss of a sense of identity.

When Jesus offered people his yoke, he was promising them more than a release from their heavy burdens, he was promising a new way of life and a new identity. So the yoke spoke about a persons identity and value system. The yoke is whatever controls and masters our lives.

Prayer: Father help me be controlled and mastered by your yoke.
Bible in a year reading plan: -1Samuel 5:1-9:10
